name: 0.45% NaCl (Half-normal saline)
moa: Hypotonic solution shifts water into ICF and ECF, lowering serum Na
aes: Cerebral edema if corrected too rapidly

name: 0.9% NaCl (Normal Saline)
moa: Expands ECF without ICF shift due to isotonic osmolality (~308 mOsm/L)
uses: Hypovolemia, sepsis, isotonic dehydration, hypernatremia volume repletion
aes: Hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis with large volumes, fluid overload

name: 3% Hypertonic saline
moa: High osmolality shifts water from ICF to ECF; raises serum Na
uses: Severe symptomatic hyponatremia, cerebral edema, elevated ICP
aes: Osmotic demyelination syndrome if corrected too quickly

name: ACE inhibitors
moa: Block Ang I→Ang II; dilate efferent arteriole

name: Acetaminophen
aes: Hepatotoxicity, papillary necrosis with chronic use

name: Acetazolamide
moa: Inhibits CA in PT, reducing HCO3/Na reabsorption
uses: Glaucoma, altitude sickness, IIH, central sleep apnea, COPD

name: Adenosine
moa: Causes afferent arteriolar constriction via A1 receptors

name: ADH/Vasopressin
moa: Binds V2 (aquaporin insertion, water reabsorption) and V1 (vasoconstriction) receptors
uses: Central DI, distributive/septic shock, bleeding esophageal varices

name: Albuterol
moa: Activates beta-2, stimulates Na-K-ATPase to shift K into cells

name: Aldosterone
moa: ↑ENaC and Na/K-ATPase in principal cells, Na reabsorption + K secretion

name: Alfuzosin
moa: Blocks α1 receptors in prostate/bladder neck

name: Allopurinol
uses: Gout, uric acid stones, tumor lysis

name: Amiloride
moa: Blocks epithelial sodium channels (ENaC) in collecting duct principal cells
uses: Nephrogenic DI from lithium, K-sparing diuresis
notes: Blocks lithium entry via Na channels

name: Aminoglycosides
moa: Bind 30S ribosome; also activate CaSR, ↓Mg reabsorption

name: Amphotericin B
moa: Binds ergosterol; ↑basolateral permeability in tubular cells

name: Aspirin
moa: Inhibits COX; uncouples oxidative phosphorylation at toxic doses
aes: Early respiratory alkalosis then mixed AGMA, tinnitus

name: Bacillus Calmette-Guerin (BCG)
moa: Induces local inflammation/immune response in bladder
aes: Cystitis, hematuria, fever, rarely disseminated BCG infection
notes: Patients will have positive TB skin test

name: Belzutifan
moa: Blocks HIF-2α, reducing transcription of hypoxia-induced genes

name: Bethanechol
moa: Stimulates M3 receptors, increases detrusor contractility
aes: Diarrhea, cramping, salivation, sweating, bronchospasm, bradycardia
notes: Caution in asthma/COPD; avoid in obstruction

name: Botulinum toxin
moa: Blocks ACh release at neuromuscular junction, paralyzes detrusor

name: Calcium chloride
notes: Higher elemental Ca; central line preferred

name: Calcium gluconate
moa: Raises threshold potential; stabilizes cardiac membrane
uses: Severe hyperkalemia with ECG changes, hypocalcemia
notes: Onset 2-5 min, does NOT lower serum K

name: Cefepime
moa: Inhibits cell wall synthesis; Pseudomonas activity

name: Cimetidine
moa: Blocks H2 receptor; inhibits tubular creatinine secretion (OCT)
aes: Elevated serum creatinine without true GFR change

name: Ciprofloxacin
moa: Inhibits DNA gyrase/topoisomerase IV; CYP3A4 inhibitor
aes: Tendon rupture, QT prolongation, CNS effects

name: Cisplatin
moa: DNA crosslinking; damages renal tubular cells

name: Conivaptan
moa: Nonselective V1/V2 ADH receptor antagonist; promotes aquaresis
aes: Orthostatic hypotension (V1 blockade), fatigue, dizziness, thirst

name: Cyclophosphamide
uses: Severe MCD, MN, anti-GBM, nephritic syndromes
aes: Hemorrhagic cystitis, infertility, bladder cancer, myelosuppression
notes: Use mesna to prevent hemorrhagic cystitis

name: Cyclosporine
moa: Inhibits calcineurin, blocking T-cell IL-2 production

name: Cystatin C
uses: Alternative GFR estimation in low muscle mass

name: D5W (5% dextrose in water)
moa: Dextrose metabolized leaving free water distributed across ICF/ECF
aes: Hyponatremia, cerebral edema if corrected too fast

name: Dactinomycin
moa: Binds guanine, intercalates between G/C, inhibits DNA/RNA/protein synthesis

name: Dapagliflozin
moa: Blocks SGLT2 in PCT, reducing glucose reabsorption
aes: UTI, genital infections, euglycemic DKA, volume depletion

name: Demeclocycline
moa: Interferes with V2 receptor signaling, prevents aquaporin translocation
notes: Causes nephrogenic DI as therapeutic effect

name: Desmopressin (DDAVP)
moa: Selective V2 agonist; promotes aquaporin insertion and water reabsorption
uses: Central DI, nocturnal enuresis, vWD, hemophilia A, water deprivation testing

name: Diazepam
moa: GABA-A positive allosteric modulator; CYP3A4 substrate

name: Dopamine
moa: Low dose: dilates renal vessels via D1; ↑RBF

name: Doxorubicin
moa: Intercalates DNA, inhibits topoisomerase II, generates free radicals

name: Enfortumab vedotin
moa: Targets Nectin-4, delivers MMAE microtubule disruptor

name: Epinephrine
moa: Beta-2 shifts K into cells; alpha shifts K out
notes: Net K effect depends on receptors

name: Erythropoietin
moa: Stimulates RBC production in bone marrow
notes: Produced by kidney; deficient in CKD

name: Ethacrynic acid
aes: More ototoxicity than other loops, hypokalemia

name: Ethanol
moa: Competes with toxic alcohols for ADH

name: Ethylene glycol
moa: Metabolized to glycolic/oxalic acid causing AGMA
aes: AGMA, renal failure, calcium oxalate stones
notes: E in MUDPILES; treat with fomepizole

name: Everolimus
moa: Inhibits mTOR serine/threonine kinase regulating cell growth
uses: Metastatic RCC; AML in tuberous sclerosis
notes: Reduces AML tumor size, preserves renal function

name: Fludrocortisone
uses: Type 4 RTA with low aldosterone, adrenal insufficiency

name: Fluoroquinolones
aes: Tendon rupture, QT prolongation, C. diff

name: Fomepizole
moa: Competitively inhibits ADH, blocks toxic alcohol metabolism
uses: Methanol, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol toxicity

name: Furosemide
moa: Blocks NKCC2 in thick ascending limb, impairing medullary gradient
uses: Edema (CHF, cirrhosis), pulmonary edema, HTN, hyperkalemia, hypercalcemia, AKI/CKD
aes: Hypokalemia, hypocalcemia, hypomagnesemia, metabolic alkalosis, ototoxicity, hyperuricemia, volume depletion
notes: Often given with saline in hypervolemic hyponatremia

name: Gemcitabine
moa: Inhibits DNA synthesis as nucleoside analog

name: Glyburide (sulfonylurea)
moa: Closes K-ATP channels in beta cells, stimulating insulin release

name: Hydrochlorothiazide
uses: HTN (1st line), calcium stones, nephrogenic DI, refractory edema
aes: Hypokalemia, hyponatremia, hypercalcemia, hyperuricemia/gout, hyperglycemia, hyperlipidemia

name: Ibuprofen
aes: AKI, AIN, papillary necrosis, GI bleed
notes: Classic NSAID cause of AIN and RPN

name: Insulin
moa: Activates insulin receptor, drives glucose into cells; stops ketogenesis

name: Interleukin-2 (Aldesleukin)
aes: Capillary leak syndrome, hypotension, arrhythmias, renal/multi-organ toxicity

name: Ketoconazole
moa: Inhibits ergosterol synthesis; potent CYP3A4 inhibitor

name: Lactated Ringer's
moa: Expands ECF; lactate is metabolized as buffer

name: Laxatives
moa: Various: stimulate or osmotic increase in GI motility

name: Lisinopril
moa: Blocks ACE, reducing angiotensin II and aldosterone

name: Lithium
moa: Interferes with ADH signaling in collecting duct; enters cells via ENaC
notes: Damage usually reversible if stopped within ~10 yr; amiloride blocks uptake

name: Magnesium
notes: Replete with K in refractory hypoK

name: Magnesium sulfate
moa: Replenishes Mg stores; cofactor for Na/K-ATPase

name: Mannitol
moa: Filtered but not reabsorbed osmole that draws water from cells/ICF into ECF and urine
uses: Cerebral edema, elevated ICP, hypertonic fluid therapy
aes: Volume depletion, pulmonary edema, hyponatremia, hypernatremia, AKI

name: Metformin
aes: GI upset, lactic acidosis, B12 deficiency

name: Methanol
moa: Metabolized by ADH to formic acid causing AGMA
notes: M in MUDPILES; treat with fomepizole

name: Methotrexate
moa: Inhibits DHFR, blocking folate metabolism and DNA synthesis

name: Methylprednisolone
uses: Anti-GBM, RPGN, severe AIN, pulse therapy

name: Mirabegron
moa: Stimulates β3 receptors on detrusor, promotes relaxation

name: Nadolol
uses: Aortic dissection risk reduction in PKD

name: Nitrofurantoin
moa: Reduced to reactive intermediates damaging bacterial DNA
aes: Pulmonary fibrosis, hepatotoxicity, neuropathy, brown urine

name: Nivolumab
moa: Blocks PD-1, restoring T-cell antitumor response

name: Normal saline
moa: Volume expansion enhances renal K/Ca excretion

name: NSAIDs
moa: Inhibit COX, blocking prostaglandin-mediated afferent vasodilation
aes: Prerenal AKI, CKD, hyperkalemia, hemorrhagic cystitis risk
notes: Avoid in CKD; cause nephrotic syndrome, AIN, analgesic nephropathy, papillary necrosis

name: Oxybutynin
moa: Blocks M3 receptors on detrusor, reduces involuntary contractions
uses: Urge incontinence, OAB, spastic neurogenic bladder
aes: Dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, urinary retention, cognitive effects

name: Oxygen
aes: O2 toxicity in COPD with chronic hypercapnia

name: Para-aminohippuric acid (PAH)
moa: Filtered and secreted by OAT; ~90% extracted

name: Patiromer
moa: Binds K in GI tract (exchanges for Ca)

name: Penicillin G
moa: Inhibits cell wall synthesis; as nonreabsorbable anion ↑distal Na delivery and K secretion
notes: Renal K wasting due to anion effect

name: Penicillins
moa: Inhibit cell wall synthesis; secreted by OAT

name: Phenylephrine
moa: Stimulates α1 receptors, contracts bladder neck/urethra smooth muscle
notes: No longer used; only mildly efficacious

name: Piperacillin-tazobactam
moa: Inhibits cell wall synthesis; tazobactam blocks beta-lactamase

name: Plasma-Lyte
moa: Physiologic-like balanced crystalloid for ECF expansion

name: Potassium acetate
uses: IV when Cl undesired; hyperchloremic acidosis

name: Potassium chloride
notes: Refractory hypoK unresponsive without Mg correction

name: Potassium citrate
uses: Hypokalemia, hypocitraturia, Ca-oxalate/uric acid stone prevention

name: Praziquantel
aes: Nausea, headache, dizziness, urticaria, transient hepatitis

name: Prednisone
uses: MCD, FSGS, MN, nephritic syndromes, AIN, autoimmune

name: Propranolol
moa: Blocks beta-1 and beta-2 adrenergic receptors
uses: Aortic dissection risk reduction in PKD

name: Propylene glycol
moa: Metabolized to lactic acid by ADH

name: Proton pump inhibitors
moa: Irreversibly inhibit gastric H/K-ATPase; also gut TRPM6/7
aes: AIN, hypomagnesemia, C. difficile, B12 deficiency

name: Sodium bicarbonate
moa: Corrects acidosis; H exits cells in exchange for K entry
uses: Metabolic acidosis, uric acid stone dissolution

name: Sodium polystyrene sulfonate (Kayexalate)
moa: Exchanges Na for K in GI tract

name: Sodium zirconium cyclosilicate
moa: Selectively traps K in GI in exchange for Na/H

name: Spironolactone
moa: Antagonizes mineralocorticoid receptor in collecting duct
aes: Hyperkalemia, gynecomastia, ↓libido, type 4 RTA, metabolic acidosis
notes: DOC for hypoK from primary hyperaldosteronism

name: Statins
uses: Hyperlipidemia of nephrotic syndrome, CV prevention

name: Tamsulosin
moa: Selectively blocks α1A receptors in prostate/bladder neck
uses: BPH, medical expulsive therapy for ureteral stones

name: Terazosin
moa: Blocks α1 receptors, relaxes smooth muscle of bladder neck/prostate
aes: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, syncope, may worsen stress incontinence

name: Thiazide diuretics
moa: Inhibit Na/Cl cotransporter in DCT; reduce urinary calcium
uses: Prevent calcium stones in medullary sponge kidney

name: Tissue plasminogen activator (tPA)
moa: Converts plasminogen to plasmin, lyses fibrin

name: Tolterodine
aes: Dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, urinary retention

name: Tolvaptan
moa: Selective V2 antagonist; blocks aquaporin insertion
aes: Polyuria, polydipsia, hypernatremia, hepatotoxicity, osmotic demyelination, thirst
notes: Avoid in liver disease; limit to <30 days outpatient; CYP3A4 substrate

name: Topical vaginal estrogen
moa: Restores urogenital epithelium and urethral mucosal coaptation
uses: Postmenopausal women with GSM + urinary urgency/frequency/mild incontinence

name: Topiramate
aes: Kidney stones, cognitive slowing, metabolic acidosis

name: Trimethoprim
moa: Inhibits bacterial DHFR; competes for tubular creatinine secretion

name: Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole
moa: TMP inhibits DHFR; SMX inhibits dihydropteroate synthase

name: Urea
moa: Filtered into nephron, increases free water excretion

name: Vancomycin
moa: Binds D-Ala-D-Ala blocking cell wall synthesis

name: Vincristine
moa: Binds tubulin, inhibits microtubule formation, arrests metaphase

name: Vitamin D
moa: Hydroxylated in PT (1α-hydroxylase) to active form
